item.title: F1 mailbag: Is Kimi Antonelli F1's Lamine Yamal? Can Ferrari close the gap to Mercedes?
item.description: From Mercedes' similarity with World Cup winners Spain to Oscar Piastri's continued struggles, our experts answer your questions

item.title: F1 is as confusing as ever. Plus: What to expect from Ferrari in Hungary
item.description: Welcome to Prime Tire, The Athletic's F1 newsletter.

item.title: George Russell may be feeling 'numb' but if he can reset, the F1 title race is not over
item.description: Russell needs to reset, while Mercedes needs to address the car's systems and reliability if he is to challenge teammate Kimi Antonelli

item.title: Lionel Messi, Argentina and the challenge of moving on from a GOAT
item.description: Plenty of teams in multiple sports have had to learn to live without a great player, some with much more success than others

item.title: Spa victory showed Kimi Antonelli is on the path to be F1's youngest champion
item.description: Antonelli has a big championship lead again after a dominant display at Spa.

item.title: Ferrari fined after Lewis Hamilton accidentally hits mechanic at Belgian Grand Prix
item.description: The result of the post-race investigation for an unsafe release means the seven-time world champion retains his fourth-placed finish at Spa.

item.title: Belgian GP: Submit your questions for our F1 mailbag
item.description: What’s on your mind after the 2026 Belgian Grand Prix? Send us your questions and we’ll answer

item.title: Belgian Grand Prix briefing: Antonelli wins, Hamilton penalty woes, Russell crashes out
item.description: Antonelli wins the Belgian Grand Prix ahead of Charles Leclerc in second, while Max Verstappen and Lewis Hamilton were third and fourth

item.title: For Franco Colapinto, Argentina’s F1 hero, the World Cup final creates more nerves than a race
item.description: Franco Colapinto has huge home support in Argentina. As he prepares to cheer on Lionel Messi and co., his F1 results are rising for Alpine

item.title: George Russell baffled by ‘serious issue’ with engine causing speed deficit to Kimi Antonelli
item.description: The Mercedes teammates and Formula 1 title rivals qualified for Sunday's race with more than half a second between them.

item.title: Why Cadillac keeps changing color. Plus: Kimi Antonelli leads early at Spa
item.description: Welcome to Prime Tire, The Athletic's F1 newsletter.

item.title: McLaren is no longer F1's dominator - but it has transformed a poor season before
item.description: The team has gone from winning the F1 world title double last year to trailing Mercedes and Ferrari in 2026. It now understands why...

item.title: Belgian GP track breakdown: Spa's iconic layout blends F1 beauty with danger
item.description: One of F1's oldest tracks, Spa is huge, fast, dangerous and utterly epic.

item.title: Oscar Piastri brushes off recent Max Verstappen McLaren links: ‘I’m very comfortable’
item.description: Verstappen’s camp approached McLaren last month for a conversation about a potential future race seat.

item.title: Red Bull drops problematic rear wing for Belgian GP after recent Max Verstappen crashes
item.description: The four-time world champion spun off-track at Stowe during the race at Silverstone, costing him a podium finish.

item.title: Ferrari, F1’s Three Lions? Plus: Deadline looms on Middle East race rescheduling
item.description: Welcome to Prime Tire, The Athletic's F1 newsletter.

item.title: Spa could be another harsh reality check for F1: 'It will probably not be a lot of fun'
item.description: Spa is a revered circuit, but because of the new regulations many drivers are predicting racing there won't have the same magic this weekend

item.title: Why 2028 is becoming Max Verstappen’s prime year for an F1 move. If he can wait
item.description: Max Verstappen's future remains a major talking point in the paddock. But his options to move away from Red Bull for 2027 are narrowing

item.title: BMW victory in 6 Hours of São Paulo gives Kevin Magnussen first World Endurance Win
item.description: BMW beats Ferrari and Cadillac to take its second victory out of four WEC races in 2026.

item.title: Three safety cars, slow zones and pit stop cameras: How endurance racing safety works
item.description: The FIA is in charge of safety in the World Endurance Championship, with its large field of cars and different categories racing at once

item.title: New boss, same problems at Red Bull? Plus: Drivers fear Spa will be 'sad'
item.description: Welcome to Prime Tire, The Athletic's F1 newsletter.

item.title: World Endurance Championship: Jamie Chadwick made history but is targeting bigger goals
item.description: After being unable to make her desired career progress on the IndyCar ladder, Jamie Chadwick has turned her attention to endurance racing

item.title: World Endurance Championship: The ‘brain trust’ fuelling Cadillac’s Le Mans dream
item.description: Cadillac will hope to make up for the disappointment of Le Mans at this weekend's 6-hour race in Sao Paulo.

item.title: British GP mailbag: Should F1 prevent safety car finishes? Why did Verstappen crash?
item.description: The British Grand Prix ended in confusion as the drivers were led home by the safety car. Our experts answer your questions about it.

item.title: Max Verstappen is ‘fed up’ with Red Bull's issues. What does it mean for his F1 future?
item.description: Things aren’t clicking as they should at Red Bull, which is only adding to the speculation around Max Verstappen's future.

item.title: Welcome to F1's new era of 'speed chess,' where every pass comes with a price
item.description: DRS is out. Overtake Mode is in. Here's why drivers say passing just got a lot harder

item.title: Why F1's boiling drama over engine compression ratios matters for the 2026 season
item.description: Has Mercedes found a loophole with its engines? And what exactly is the loophole, anyway?

item.title: F1's new cars behave unlike any others. It's all down to new aerodynamics
item.description: F1 has new cars in 2026, which look different to previous eras in grand prix racing. Here's how they work and what the drivers think

item.title: F1 hits reset in 2026. Your guide to the new cars, new rules and new era
item.description: Formula 1 evolves dramatically in 2026. Here's your year-long guide to it all

item.title: F1's new engines are the sport's biggest gamble in 12 years. Here's what changed
item.description: F1 is introducing new hybrid engines ahead of the 2026 season. Here's how they work and why they matter

item.title: How Formula 1's cost cap works: Rules, penalties and past breaches explained
item.description: The sport's $135 million spending limit aims to create parity — but compliance isn't always simple

item.title: F1 penalty points: How they work and why they're needed even though drivers have doubts
item.description: A deep dive into the details - and arguments - of Formula One penalty points for driver infractions

item.title: What are F1 driver development programs? Inside the academies that nurture future champions
item.description: All of F1's new 2025 drivers have one thing in common - they were part of a team’s driver development program or academy.

item.title: What are F1’s racing rules? A look at race stewards and drivers' battle for clarity
item.description: Multiple times during the 2024 season, drivers have faced questions — and raised their own — about what is and is not allowed on track.

item.title: How F1's Red Bull mastered the art of the 2-second pit stop
item.description: A deep dive into the positions, the practice, and the fine-tuned production that is the Formula One pit stop.

item.title: Welcome to Between the Racing Lines, your new guide to all things F1
item.description: F1 is complicated and confusing at times, and is constantly evolving. Here's your guide to keeping up.

item.title: Sim time, data crunching and Candy Crush: The life of an F1 reserve driver
item.description: Reserve drivers are more than simple substitutes — they play important roles within their teams, even if they rarely get to hit the track.

item.title: How F1 drivers train physically and mentally to go from 'gym fit' to 'race fit'
item.description: To have a chance at the title, drivers need not just a top-caliber car but to extract the maximum performance from it — and themselves.
